Title: Genetic analysis of seed traits in Sorghum bicolor that affect the human gut microbiome

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: IS3620C (wild-type allele); BTx623 carries tan1-b loss-of-function allele
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BTx623 × IS3620C recombinant inbred lines; near-isogenic lines; parents BTx623 and IS3620C
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed testa layer; can spread into pericarp in suitable genetic backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates human gut microbiome fermentation; stimulates beneficial gut microbes such as Faecalibacterium prausnitzii; also associated with bird predation resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type Tan1 promotes condensed tannin accumulation and brown seed color when combined with wild-type Tan2, leading to increased abundances of multiple gut microbial taxa in fermentation assays; tan1-b loss-of-function abolishes tannin production and associated microbiome effects
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of polyphenol/flavonoid biosynthesis, especially proanthocyanidin/condensed tannin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation of genes in the polyphenol/flavonoid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes in the polyphenol/flavonoid pathway, including proanthocyanidin and anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required positive regulation of condensed tannin/proanthocyanidin pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential regulator; wild-type Tan1 is required together with wild-type Tan2 for condensed tannin synthesis in seed
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Implied regulator complex function with Tan2 in controlling tannin pathway; direct complex not experimentally demonstrated here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate gene highly expressed in seed context; study discusses seed-associated function
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro digestion and anaerobic fermentation with human fecal microbiomes; tannin complementation with sorghum or quebracho tannin extracts
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL co-localization with seed color, tannin content, and microbiome traits; haplotype segregation analysis in RILs; validation in near-isogenic lines; metabolite quantification of tannins; molecular complementation with purified condensed tannins; 16S rRNA profiling; species-specific qPCR for Faecalibacterium prausnitzii
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA gene sequencing, NCBI SRA PRJNA801694; candidate-gene mining used RNA-seq expression data from Phytozome v13 and public resequencing data
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tan1 co-localizes with a major-effect locus on chromosome 4 affecting seed color, tannin content, and abundances of eight gut microbial taxa. 2. Wild-type Tan1 is required, together with wild-type Tan2, for condensed tannin synthesis and brown seed phenotype in the RIL population. 3. Tannin-positive lines carrying functional Tan1/Tan2 increase abundances of Faecalibacterium, Roseburia, Christensenellaceae R7 group and related taxa in human microbiome fermentations. 4. Loss-of-function tan1-b in BTx623 blocks tannin accumulation and associated microbiome effects.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Condensed tannins are associated with bitter taste and were historically selected against during domestication; no direct yield penalty quantified in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for breeding sorghum lines with elevated condensed tannins to beneficially modulate the gut microbiome; deployment should consider taste/consumer acceptance and potential trade-offs with agronomic and sensory traits; stacking functional Tan1 with Tan2 is necessary
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BTx623 carries the recessive tan1-b allele with a 10-bp insertion in the C-terminal exon that truncates 35 amino acids. Functional Tan1 alone is insufficient; both Tan1 and Tan2 are required due to duplicate recessive epistasis.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: BTx623 (wild-type allele); IS3620C carries tan2-c loss-of-function allele
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BTx623 × IS3620C recombinant inbred lines; parents BTx623 and IS3620C
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed testa layer; visible as brown layer and in flour when present
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates human gut microbiome fermentation; stimulates beneficial taxa through condensed tannin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type Tan2 promotes condensed tannin accumulation and brown seed color when combined with wild-type Tan1, leading to increased abundances of multiple gut microbial taxa; tan2-c loss-of-function blocks this effect
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of polyphenol/flavonoid biosynthesis, especially proanthocyanidin/condensed tannin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation of genes in the polyphenol/flavonoid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes in the polyphenol/flavonoid pathway, including proanthocyanidin and anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required positive regulation of condensed tannin/proanthocyanidin pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential regulator; wild-type Tan2 is required together with wild-type Tan1 for condensed tannin synthesis in seed
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Implied combinatorial action with Tan1 for pathway activation; direct complex not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate gene highly expressed in seed context; study discusses seed-associated function
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro digestion and anaerobic fermentation with human fecal microbiomes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL co-localization with seed color, tannin content, and microbiome traits; haplotype segregation analysis in RILs; tannin quantification; 16S rRNA profiling; species-specific qPCR validation for Faecalibacterium prausnitzii
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA gene sequencing, NCBI SRA PRJNA801694; candidate-gene mining used RNA-seq expression data from Phytozome v13 and public resequencing data
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tan2 co-localizes with a major-effect locus on chromosome 2 affecting seed color, tannin content, and multiple microbial taxa. 2. Wild-type Tan2 inherited from BTx623, together with wild-type Tan1 from IS3620C, explains transgressive tannin-positive and brown-seeded RIL phenotypes. 3. Tannin-positive Tan1/Tan2 lines show increased abundances of Faecalibacterium, Roseburia, Christensenellaceae R7 group, Coprococcus 1 and others. 4. The tan2-c allele in IS3620C abolishes tannin accumulation despite functional Tan1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As with Tan1, higher tannin may contribute to bitterness and possible acceptance issues; no direct yield trade-off quantified
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful breeding target in combination with Tan1 for developing microbiome-beneficial, high-tannin sorghum; must retain functional alleles at both loci
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IS3620C carries the recessive tan2-c allele with a 95-bp deletion removing the entire intron between exons 7 and 8. Functional Tan2 alone is insufficient because both Tan1 and Tan2 are required for pathway output.
